expected: Two concurrent read requests for the same unread notification both return 200.
Both responses expose the same non-null readAt timestamp; the losing request returns the already-read row instead of overwriting readAt.
Unread summary becomes zero and readback status=read contains one notification row.
nonGoals: No production behavior change was needed; existing repository idempotency was verified with fresh concurrent DB evidence.

evidence.testAdded: apps/api/src/collaborationRoutes.db.test.ts: keeps notification read timestamps stable on concurrent read requests
evidence.readback: Test asserts status=read returns exactly one target notification with the same readAt.
evidence.warning: Existing duplicate assignment.delete warning in packages/domain/src/planning/commandReducer.ts remains unrelated.

fix.behavior: Coverage-only slice. Production code already updates only unread rows and returns the existing row for duplicate reads.

remainingRisk: Other write-flow duplicate/race/idempotency slices remain open: communication channel rename, auth reset confirm/email delivery, CRM conflicts, access-role name DB uniqueness, and remaining write flows.
